🚀 V1 Launch Spec — Condensed Core Loop
Mar 22 2026 · Per Mark: "Easy to use, simple, no friction, addictive. Prioritize and sequence so we can roll out a little bit at a time." The entire V1 app is these 3 screens. Home → Pick Card → Pick Result. Key simplification vs. existing screens: multiplier is INLINE on the pick card (toggle, not a bottom sheet). One screen, two taps, you're in. V2 adds social. V3 adds intelligence. V4 adds venue.
LAUNCH SEQUENCE
V1 · Launch
• Onboarding (3 taps)
• Home (signal + streak + rank)
• Pick Card (inline multiplier)
• Pick Result (win/loss)
• Notifications ask
• Pro soft paywall
V2 · Social
• Full leaderboard tab
• Referral + share card
• Friends feed tab
• Bar Mode check-in
• Challenges / H2H
• Daily Challenge tab
V3 · Intelligence
• Prop Coach (Pro)
• Sports DNA / archetypes
• Sharp Digest (Pro)
• Full Stats screen
• Trivia
V4 · Venue
• Venue onboarding
• Bar dashboard
• Admin in-app
• B2B revenue layer

TRIVIA v2 · 2026-03-21 · UPDATED PER FEEDBACK — continuous mode · 6 sports · SportsIQ triggers bar rounds
🧠 Sports Trivia — The Daily Hook
Trivia fires every day, even when no game is live. It's the second addiction loop alongside live picks. Continuous mode — no fixed question count, play as long as you want. Every question answered adds to your personal Trivia IQ stat across all 6 sports (NFL, NBA, NHL, MLB, College FB, College BB) — a proprietary accuracy score no competitor can replicate. Between game prediction windows at bars, SportsIQ automatically triggers trivia rounds on the bar screen — venues don't manage it, it just happens seamlessly.

🏰 MOAT ANALYSIS — TRIVIA
Daily engagement loop: Trivia fires every day — even during offseason. 5 questions = 3 min = daily habit. Streak mechanic (same as Duolingo) drives returns even when no game is scheduled.
Proprietary data asset: Your Trivia IQ score (847 / 1000) is unique to SportsIQ. 6 months of trivia history is something a user will not abandon for a competitor that starts them at zero.
Bar TV integration: Venue can run a trivia round between pick windows (halftime, between innings). 5 questions × 4 rounds = 20 questions per venue visit = stronger retention than picks alone.
SportsIQ question bank moat: SportsIQ's question bank improves from usage data — which questions are too easy/hard, which drive engagement, which get shared. After 100K questions answered, the bank becomes a data asset competitors can't buy.

WHAT IS SPORTS DNA?
Your Sports DNA is built automatically from every pick you make. Over time it reveals your fan profile: which sports you know best, how you pick under pressure, whether you follow the crowd or go contrarian. It's a living profile that gets more accurate the more you play.

Crew Challenge — H2H + Group · Unified · 2026-03-22
One feature for both 1v1 and groups. Add 1 opponent = Head-to-Head. Add 2–5 = Group Battle. Same prop, same window, same IQ stakes — winner earns real IQ from all losers. Works asynchronously (each player picks in their own window). No separate Group feature needed.

🏰 MOAT ANALYSIS — HEAD-TO-HEAD CHALLENGE
Retention mechanic with no parallel: A challenge from a specific friend is a personal social contract — ignoring it creates social friction. This forces daily opens even on non-game days. DraftKings has no equivalent social layer; their "contests" are anonymous. SportsIQ's direct challenges create named rivalries — a fundamentally different (and stickier) dynamic.
Organic acquisition engine: Every challenge sent is an implicit invite. If the recipient isn't a SportsIQ user, they must install to respond. Cost of acquisition: $0. The challenger is doing the recruiting. At scale, 10% of challenges going to non-users = meaningful organic install loop.
H2H record as switching cost: After 20 challenges with Mike, your 4-4 H2H record is a data asset you'd lose by switching apps. Rivalries accumulate history; history creates lock-in.
IQ transfer as status currency: Losing 150 IQ to Mike in a direct challenge is more painful (and thus motivating) than losing 150 IQ in an anonymous crowd contest. Status effects are amplified when the opponent is named.
My Stats v3 — Context-Aware Data Surfacing · Right Stat at Right Time
Not everything always. 3 context states: (1) Default — essentials only, clean layout. (2) Pre-game — your stats for the specific player/prop in the upcoming pick window. (3) H2H preview — your H2H record vs upcoming opponent, surfaced only when that match is near. Same data, smarter timing.

🏰 MOAT ANALYSIS — BAR LIVE PICK v4
Proprietary crowd data is the product: By removing Polymarket/Kalshi from the bar screen, SportsIQ crowd becomes the only signal in the room. The 68/32 split is SportsIQ's data — generated by SportsIQ users, owned by SportsIQ. Every person who stares at that screen and picks OVER is generating behavioral data that Kalshi can't see. The bar TV is a data-collection flywheel disguised as entertainment.
The bar screen is SportsIQ's acquisition machine: Every non-user watching the bar screen is one scan away from installing the app. The QR + 5-digit room code gives two paths to join — camera-first and manual. At 50 bars with 30 spectators each, that's 1,500 potential installs per night. No paid CAC.
The "2× bar multiplier" creates location lock-in: Users who discover they earn twice the points when playing at a bar venue will prefer playing at bars over playing alone at home. This drives foot traffic to venues (venue owner benefit) while increasing SportsIQ engagement per session. DraftKings has no equivalent location-aware multiplier.
BAR SCREEN v4 — IDLE · Dynamic 3-column layout · Auto-curated · No owner input · 2026-03-22 (wider left panel, room code on one line)

Bar · Tracking v3 — Useful Stats · Result State
Two states: Live tracking (stat vs line, live leaderboard) and Result revealed (win/loss, updated positions, next pick teaser). Today-only — clears 4am ET. Late arrivals climb via win-rate, not total IQ.

Bar Screens — QR Standard · Bottom-Right Corner · Consistent Across All 7 Screens
Apr 3 2026 v4 · QR shrunk slightly (18px horizontal padding on all 7 screens) to give more leaderboard length — identical on all screens.
▦
74291
QR STANDARD — ALL BAR SCREENS
• 130×130px QR on 1080p layout = ~3.8 inches on a 65" TV — scannable from 10–15 ft
• position:absolute · bottom:28px · right:28px — pixel-identical on all 7 screens
• Corner overlay, not a column — trivia screens freed 220px of wasted side column
• White background for maximum contrast in dark bar environments
• Room code as fallback for guests who don't want to scan
• Zero bartender action — QR and code are static per session

Trivia v3 — TV Screen · 10s Countdown · 5s Reveal
Two states: Question live (10s countdown bar, 4 options, tap-to-lock) and Answer revealed (5s, correct option highlighted, SportsIQ Insight, auto-advance). 100% SportsIQ-curated. ~70% correct rate target. No lock-in button, no next-question button.

NFL · STATS & RECORDS
How many touchdown passes did Patrick Mahomes throw in the 2024 NFL regular season?

❌

✅
CORRECT

❌
📊 SportsIQ Insight
Mahomes threw 52 TDs in 2024 — only Brady (50), Rodgers (45) and Peyton (49) are even close in a single season. He was on pace for a 6th MVP before getting dinged in Week 16.

HOME v5 — LOCKED · Button Gone · Confirmation Card
Pick Card v6 — Sharp Money Intelligence · Simplified 1×/2×/5× · Concrete Pro Math
2026-03-22 · Design target: must work for a 15yr old sports fan, a 35yr old non-sports female, AND a die-hard 40yr old male. (1) Plain-language explainer added below prop line — "Will Mahomes throw for more or fewer than 284 yards? His last 5 games averaged 312 — meaning he's been above this line often lately." Non-sports fans can now make an informed pick without knowing what a passing yard is. Die-hards still get the full stats. (2) Sharp Money split: Public % vs. Sharp % side-by-side — electric for die-hards, blurred for free users → Pro conversion. (3) 1×/2×/5× multiplier. (4) Concrete Pro math — "You'd win +425 more IQ with Pro." The plain-language explainer is the accessibility unlock that turns this app from a sports app into a trivia/prediction app anyone can play.

Onboarding v6 — V1 Spec · Sport Select → Pick Fires → Name After Hook
Mar 22 2026 · V1 simplicity directive. Three interactions before you're in the game: (1) Pick your sport. (2) Pick OVER or UNDER. (3) What's your name (after the first result). That's it. No social proof splash, no tutorial, no account wall. TikTok principle: show the experience first, then ask for commitment.

Daily Challenge v4 — Actual Picks · 6pm Local Expiry · Per-Pick Immediate Results
3 key changes from v3: (1) Share card shows your actual OVER/UNDER picks — not Wordle dots. (2) Challenge expires at 6pm in user's local time — morning-to-afternoon urgency window. (3) Results fire immediately after each individual pick resolves, not batch at end. 4 states shown.

Friends Feed v2 — Pending Picks First · 4-Emoji Reactions · Manual Follow
Four explicit changes from Mark's idea-071: (1) Feed shows picks the instant they're locked, before result settles — anticipation is the hook. (2) Every item has a 4-emoji reaction row (🔥👀😬✅) — simple, no threads. (3) Manual follow only — nothing auto-followed, users choose who they track. (4) All friends shown — "Following 14 · All" with zero algorithmic filtering.

Phone 3 — Crew Leaderboard: ranked crews global + weekly
Share Card v3 — Archetype Identity · Venue Rank · Instagram Story Format
2026-03-22 · Three new card types that generate the most shares and drive the most new users. (1) Archetype Identity Card: fires when your Sports DNA archetype matched the game and you won. "🏟️ The Dome King called it again" is a deeply personal win that users want to brag about — it shows off their sports identity, not just a number. Converts viewers into "wait, what's my archetype?" users. (2) Venue Rank Brag Card: "#1 at Oaks Bar tonight" geo-localized social proof. This card drives two things simultaneously: new user downloads AND foot traffic to partner bars. A viewer who sees this wants to come to the bar and play. This is the bar venue flywheel made visible. (3) Instagram Story Format: same cards but formatted as 9:16 with top/bottom safe zones, large typography readable at story size, and a "Download SportsIQ" bottom CTA. Stories get 3-5× more impressions than feed posts — this format is how we go viral.

Phone 3 — Instagram Story format: full-bleed 9:16 · "Find your Sports DNA →" CTA · 12,400 social proof · Download button · Stories get 3-5× more impressions than feed posts

⚡ Active Picks Tray — Overlapping Cadence · Micro-Picks · Live Game Banner

Mar 22 2026 · The core engagement architecture. Two pick types run simultaneously: (1) Game-level picks (2–4hr) — Chiefs ML, Mahomes OVER 284.5 — sit live in a tray while the game plays. (2) Micro-picks (5–7 min) — "Will the next drive score?" — fast-fire, always fresh, resolve and refresh automatically. You always have something resolving in minutes AND something sitting live for hours. No cap on active picks for any user — free or Pro. Pro value: 5× multiplier (vs 2× free), Sharp Money intel, Edge Conditions in DNA. Four screens: (1) Home with Active Picks tray — free user, 3 live picks, new pick CTA open to all. (2) Micro-pick card — Yes/No and Over/Under formats, 1×/2× stakes. (3) Pro state — 4 simultaneous picks, win flash with share hook + streak. (4) Brutal beat — loss state with near-miss stat and "Share the pain" hook.

👥 Social Proof on Picks — Friend + Bar Layers

Mar 22 2026 · Three layers of social signal shown on every pick card. (1) Friends first — most personal, strongest influence. Shows specific first names + pick direction. (2) Bar layer — appears when user is checked in at a venue. "4 people at Yard House picked OVER." Location-aware and only visible in context. (3) Global crowd — background signal, least personal. Hierarchy matters: friends override crowd because social proof from people you know is 10× more persuasive than strangers. Switching cost: the longer you use SportsIQ, the more your friend network picks here — making it increasingly painful to leave. Bar layer creates a venue-specific micro-community that no other app can replicate without SportsIQ's check-in infrastructure.

📤 Win Share Card — Instant 1-Tap at Resolve

Mar 22 2026 · Appears the instant a micro-pick resolves as a WIN. Optimized for speed — one big button, zero friction. The share card (what gets posted) is pre-built and waiting; user just hits Share and picks where to send it. Three views: (1) In-app win resolve overlay — the moment it hits. (2) The share card graphic itself — clean dark branded card, looks great on stories and feeds. (3) Bar check-in prompt — how the app surfaces "You're near Yard House — want to check in?" before picking, so bar layer activates naturally.

🏠 Home Screen — Progressive Disclosure

Mar 22 2026 · The master layout Isaac builds everything from. Core design principle: casual users see ONE thing. Depth surfaces only as context becomes relevant. A brand-new user sees a single micro-pick card and nothing else — no active tray, no social feed, no complexity. Features appear progressively as users earn them through engagement: picks tray after their first pick, social layer after adding a friend, bar layer only when checked in, Pro features blurred at the bottom as ambient aspiration. Three phones: (1) New user — day 1, totally clean, one big pick card. (2) Active user — 1 week in, picks tray + social proof, still calm. (3) Power user / Pro — full layout, all layers visible, Sharp Money + streak.

Design principle — Progressive disclosure
Day 1 — New user
One micro-pick. Nothing else. The pick IS the whole app right now. Casual users don't see features they haven't unlocked yet — they see the fun.
Week 1 — Active user
Active picks tray appears (they have picks live). Friend layer shows once they've added someone. Bar layer appears only when near a venue. Still calm.
Month 1+ — Power user
Sharp Money, Edge Conditions, Sports DNA all visible but blurred for free. They've earned the complexity by using the app — it doesn't feel overwhelming because they grew into it.

🏠 Home — Week 1 State + Daily Challenge Card (P4.10)

Mar 22 2026 · Isaac P4.10 reference: shows exact placement of Daily Challenge Card on the home screen. Week 1 state (days 4–7): compact picks tray is now showing, friend activity row has appeared, Daily Challenge Card sits below the featured pick. Layout order from top: (1) header with IQ chip → taps to Settings Modal. (2) Featured pick card. (3) Daily Challenge Card — "Play Today's Challenge 🧠" — shows daily status (played or not), IQ at stake, streak. (4) Active picks tray (compact, 2 live picks). (5) Friend activity strip ("Jake went OVER"). Phone 2 shows the Daily Challenge Card detail view. The card disappears from home once played today — doesn't clutter the screen after interaction.

🔗 Referral Landing Page — lumen-iq.com/ref/[username]

Mar 22 2026 · When a user taps a SportsIQ share card link (e.g. from "Called it. 🎯" share), they land on this mobile web page at lumen-iq.com/ref/marcd. The page knows who referred them, shows their stats/moment to create social proof and FOMO, then drives directly to App Store download. Two views: (1) Mobile web landing page — full viewport on phone browser, referrer context prominently shown, live pick teaser, download CTA. (2) Desktop/iPad version — wider layout, same core content. The referral link carries the username so the referrer gets credited and the new user is auto-connected as a friend on first launch. The page should convert in under 5 seconds — immediate hook, minimal friction to download.

🏆 Leaderboard — 4 Tabs: Friends · My Bar · Venue · Global

Mar 22 2026 · Isaac P4.7 reference. The Leaderboard tab (Social tab in nav) has 4 sub-tabs: Friends | My Bar | Venue | Global. Four phones showing each tab's active state. Friends: ranked list of people you follow — shows their pick today, IQ delta, head-to-head record. My Bar: checked-in players at your current venue — ephemeral, resets each visit, Bar Champion badge. Venue: empty state when not checked in — "Check in at a bar" CTA. Global: top pickers globally this week — weekly IQ rank, Pro ⚡ badges. Each tab has distinct social utility. Pro ⚡ badge shows on all tabs.

🚀 Onboarding Flow — First Pick in Under 60 Seconds

Mar 22 2026 · Updated for broad appeal: must work for a 15yr old sports fan, a 35yr old non-sports female, AND a 40yr old die-hard male. Five-step activation funnel. Steps: (1) Welcome — "Pick smarter. Beat your friends. Win the bar." No jargon. (2) "What sounds fun to you?" — reframed from "which sports do you follow?" Includes 🌟 "Not really a sports person — surprise me!" option for non-fans. Selecting it flags guided mode: SportsIQ picks for you, plain-English on every prop. Die-hards pick their leagues as before. (3) First live pick — plain-language explainer included. (4) Reveal moment — win or lose, dopamine hit. (5) Friends invite — viral loop. No account required until after step 4.

STEP 1 — WELCOME · ~5 sec
SportsIQ
Pick smarter. Beat your friends.
Win the bar.
Let's play →
No account needed to start
Tagline covers all three loops: personal, social, venue. No sign-up gate — friction-free entry.
STEP 2 — YOUR SPORTS · ~10 sec
Step 1 of 4
What sounds
fun to you?
Pick as many as you want — or let us surprise you
🏈NFL
🏀NBA
⚾MLB
🏒NHL
⚽Soccer
🏌️Golf
🥊MMA
🌟
Not really a sports person — surprise me!
SportsIQ will guide your picks · plain English, always
Next →
"What sounds fun to you?" — not "which sports do you follow?" Non-fans select 🌟 Surprise me → gets all sports, guided mode, plain-English explainers on every pick. Die-hards pick their leagues. Same next step either way.
STEP 3 — YOUR FIRST PICK · ~20 sec
⚡ Micro-Pick

Micro-pick: resolves in ~5 min, instant gratification. Countdown ring creates urgency from second one. No tutorial — the pick IS the onboarding. Win = dopamine hit before step 4.

🎯
Called it. ✓

+50 IQ
First win 🎉

who picked the same game.

Dopamine moment. Whether they win or lose — the reveal is exciting. "You beat 61%" creates instant status. CTA naturally flows into step 5.
STEP 5 — INVITE FRIENDS · ~10 sec

Contacts pre-loaded. One tap to invite all — or selectively. "See how your friends did" ties directly back to the win moment. Viral loop triggers here.

🍺 Bar Leaderboard — Live Venue Rankings

Mar 22 2026 · The bar layer in full. Once checked in at a venue, a live leaderboard shows every SportsIQ player at that bar tonight — ranked by IQ earned since check-in. Top player earns the Bar Champion 👑 badge for the night. Badge is contextual — only visible when the champion is physically at that bar, never on their global profile. Ephemeral authority: you're the king of this room, right now. Resets every visit, so the crown is always up for grabs. Three views: (1) The bar leaderboard itself — live rankings, your position highlighted, Bar Champion at top. (2) Bar Champion badge on the pick card — shows only while checked in at that bar. (3) End-of-night recap — your performance, shareable card, rivalry hook for next visit.

🍺 Bar Download Flow — QR Scan → Phone Auth → First Pick → Leaderboard

Mar 22 2026 · A completely different entry point from normal onboarding. Someone at a bar scans a QR code (on a table tent, TV graphic, or bar screen), downloads the app, and opens it. The app already knows they're at Yard House. Phone number verification via Twilio is required upfront (already built). Five steps, ~45 seconds scan-to-leaderboard: (1) Bar landing — venue-aware, social proof, "Get in the game →". (2) Phone auth — enter number, receive SMS code, verify. Twilio, already built. Framed as "get in the game" not "create account." (3) Username — "Name your spot on the board." No email beyond phone auth. (4) Live game micro-pick — straight to the Chiefs game on the bar TV. (5) Leaderboard — instantly on the board. The Twilio step adds ~20 seconds but every bar scan becomes a real verified user — not a guest session. Bar owner gets a permanent, qualified install. Worth it.

📍Yard House · detected
The game is on.
Everyone at this bar
is already picking.
14 players · Chiefs vs Eagles

Quick verify · just your phone number · takes ~45 sec
QR code carries venue ID. App opens knowing it's Yard House — no location permission prompt needed. Social proof is immediate: 14 real people, already picking.

Phone number = permanent verified user, not a guest. Every bar scan becomes a real install. Venue context stays visible during auth so the FOMO pressure stays on. Already built via Twilio — this is just showing it in the flow.

👥 Crews — Groups with a Leaderboard and a Moat

Mar 23 2026 · Crews are private pick groups. Pro users create them; free users can join but not create. Three views: (1) Browse/join screen — how free users discover and join crews. (2) Create a Crew — Pro-only gate that converts at the right moment of social desire. (3) Active crew view — leaderboard, live pick feed, crew IQ, and rivalry challenges within the group. Moat mechanics: the more friends in your crew, the more painful it is to switch apps. Crew IQ is distinct from weekly IQ — it's a permanent scoreboard within the group that never resets, accumulating group identity over time. Crew challenges between two crews create appointment viewing and viral rivalry moments.

💰 IQ Ways — Every Way You Earn, Free vs Pro

Mar 22 2026 · The "More scoring opportunities" Pro perk made concrete. Three views: (1) Hub — all IQ earning categories in one place, free vs Pro comparison with locked Pro-only bonuses creating visible FOMO. (2) Referral IQ feed — you earn IQ in real time when people you referred make winning picks. Passive income loop that makes sharing feel profitable, not charitable. (3) Weekly milestone tracker — streak of daily targets that drive pick activity every day of the week, not just game days. Moat: the more IQ sources a user is actively earning from, the more invested they become and the higher the switching cost. Pro unlocks 4 exclusive earning channels that free users can see but not access.

👤 Profile — Your SportsIQ Identity

Mar 22 2026 · Three views: (1) Your own profile — Lifetime IQ as the hero number, Sports DNA archetype, personal win record, active crew memberships, weekly rank. (2) Public profile — what other users see when they tap your name in the leaderboard or social feed. (3) Lifetime IQ milestone track — visual progress toward unlock thresholds (placeholder: exact rewards TBD but the track itself drives retention). The profile is the "why I'm invested" screen — the place that makes a user feel their SportsIQ history is real and worth protecting. Switching cost: all your Lifetime IQ, DNA history, crew rank, and streak record is right here, and none of it transfers.

Each milestone is a contest tier key. 1K → basic ($50), 5K → mid ($250), 10K → premium ($500+), 25K → monthly mega, 50K → elite invitational. Free users can earn their way into premium contests. 59% to 25K = active pull to keep playing.

🏆 IQ Prize Contests — Best Pickers Win Real Prizes

Mar 22 2026 · IQ points now have tangible value: they're your entry ticket to skill-based prize contests. The best pickers during a contest window win real prizes — gift cards, SportsIQ merch, Pro time. Completely legal in all 50 states (skill-based, not random). SportsIQ provides prizes from subscription revenue. No IQ is spent or wagered — your IQ balance qualifies you to enter, and your picks during the contest window determine if you win. Pro users unlock bigger contests with better prizes. Three views: (1) Contest Hub — browsing open contests, free vs Pro tiers visible. (2) Inside an active contest — live rank, your picks, leaderboard. (3) Contest win notification — you won, here's your prize, share the moment. The moat: prize contests give IQ a real-world meaning that competitors can't easily replicate without the same prize budget. Every contest is a push notification event that drives re-engagement.

🧠 Trivia Gameplay — Daily Challenge Flow

Mar 22 2026 · Updated: trivia categories BROADENED beyond sports (Mark confirmed). Categories: Sports (NFL/NBA/MLB/NHL/Soccer) AND Pop Culture, Movies/TV, Music, Current Events. A 35yr old who never watches sports can win at trivia using her own knowledge — both paths feed the same IQ economy and contest eligibility. This is the non-sports entry point that makes the app work for everyone. The daily 5-question round mixes categories — a die-hard gets sports questions, a casual fan might get pop culture. Both earn IQ, both compete on the leaderboard. Three phone views: (1) Live question — 5-question daily round, 15-second timer, 4 answers. IQ = +15 base + seconds remaining. (2) Answer reveal — correct/wrong, speed breakdown, streak badge. (3) Round complete 5/5 — +113 IQ, per-question breakdown, share CTA. (4) Wrong answer — +0 IQ, streak broken, educational fact, "recover some IQ" copy. Isaac P4.10 reference.

Adrian Peterson's single-game record is 296 yards (2007). Jamal Lewis set the record in 2003 with 295 yards — both are in the top 3 all-time.

Wrong answer: +0 IQ, streak broken. User's pick vs correct answer shown side-by-side. "Did you know" fact softens the miss and makes it educational. "Recover some IQ" copy on Next button keeps momentum going.

👥 Social Feed — Real-Time Friend Picks

Mar 22 2026 · The Social tab — a live feed of what your friends are picking right now. Not a static leaderboard; a scrollable activity stream showing picks as they happen, resolve as they end, and call-outs when a friend disagrees with you. Three views: (1) The feed itself — friend pick cards in real time, with split indicators when they go opposite you. (2) A friend's pick detail — expand any card to see their full pick, reasoning, and pick on the opposite side. (3) Bar-mode feed — when checked in, bar picks surface in an amber layer above the friend feed. Moat: the feed gets richer the more friends you have on SportsIQ — every friend added makes the product more valuable and more painful to leave.

🔔 Push Notifications — What Fires and When

Mar 22 2026 · Every notification is a re-engagement moment. Five trigger types shown across two phones: (1) Micro-pick resolve — win or loss, fires within seconds of result. (2) Friend disagrees — "Sam went the other way 👀" creates instant rivalry investment. (3) Streak milestone — "🔥 5 in a row" makes the streak feel real. (4) Daily pick available — morning habit trigger. (5) Bar proximity — "14 people at Yard House are picking right now" when you're nearby. Design rule: every notification must deliver value or create FOMO — nothing generic, nothing spammy.

📱 LOCK SCREEN — NOTIFICATION STACK
9:41
Sunday, March 22
🎯
SportsIQ now
Called it. +50 IQ 🎯
Chiefs scored · YES · Tap to share
S
SportsIQ 2m ago
Sam went the other way 👀
Mahomes UNDER · You're on opposite sides
🔥
SportsIQ 5m ago
🔥 5 in a row. You're on fire.
Keep it going — next pick is live
🍺
SportsIQ 8m ago
14 players at Yard House picking
DanTheMan is winning the bar tonight 👑
Every notif is specific — the pick, the friend's name, the streak number. Generic "You have a new pick" trains users to ignore. Specific = opens.
📱 IN-APP BANNERS + TIMING RULES
9:41 AM🔋
SportsIQ
S
Sam went UNDER 👀
Opposite side from you on Mahomes
✕
NOTIFICATION TIMING RULES
🎯 Micro-pick resolved
Fires: instantly · Lock screen + in-app banner
Win → green · Loss → red brutal beat variant
👀 Friend picks opposite
Fires: within 30 sec of their pick locking
Only if you're already in the same pick
🔥 Streak milestone
Fires: at 3, 5, 10, 20 wins in a row
Never fires mid-game — waits for clean resolve
☀️ Daily pick available
Fires: 8:00 AM local time · once per day max
Skipped if user already picked that morning
🍺 Near a bar with active players
Fires: once per venue per day · 3+ players inside
Shows player count + current bar champion
🏠Home
🎯Pick
👥Social
👤Profile
Timing rules prevent spam: daily pick fires once, bar proximity once per venue per day, streak only at milestone numbers. Volume stays low enough that every notification feels meaningful.

⚡ Pro Upgrade Funnel — Trigger Moments → Paywall

Mar 22 2026 · Pro is never pushed cold — it's always triggered by a moment of desire: trying to use 5× multiplier, seeing blurred Edge Conditions, wanting Sharp Money intel. Three trigger screens + the paywall itself: (1) Multiplier gate — user taps 5× on a pick card. (2) Sharp Money gate — blurred intel on pick card. (3) The paywall — full value stack with all 7 confirmed Pro perks: 5× multiplier, Sharp Money Intel, Edge Conditions, ⚡ badge, Crew Creation (free users can only join groups), Pro-only contests, more scoring opportunities. Annual anchor pricing shown first, 7-day trial CTA. No annoying pop-ups, no nag banners — Pro surfaces only when the user reaches for it.

Home Idle v6 — Bar Regular Archetype · Identity-Forward Personalization
Mar 22 2026 · idea-079 · Mark: "bar regular is fun · embedded in home idle screen · just experience the personalization." Archetype (🏟️ The Dome King) is auto-generated from pick history and surfaced as the hero card on home idle — above the fold, no explanation of how it was built. Phone 1: archetype card between games (private, Pro-locked breakdown teaser). Phone 2: bar check-in recognizes you as a regular — "The Dome King is here · #2 at this bar." Phone 3: archetype evolution notification in-app when your pattern shifts.

Phone 3 — Archetype evolution: ❄️ Cold Weather Skeptic → 🏟️ The Dome King · 2 new patterns locked behind Pro
⚡ SportsIQ Pro v3
Mar 22 2026 · Strategic refinement: v2 had 8 perks in a grid — too much cognitive load at the moment of decision. v3 applies the V1 "simple, no friction" rule to the paywall itself. Three contextual triggers, each with a SINGLE personal hook instead of a feature list. The Prop Coach quote references real user data ("Your Dome King edge"). Conversion science says single-benefit paywalls outperform feature grids by 30-40%. No change to price ($4.99) or perks — just a radically cleaner presentation.
🏟️
DOME KING EDGE ACTIVE
5× is locked for free users

Mobile App — 3-Screen Architecture v1 · Pick · Result · Leaderboard · 2026-03-22
Complete redesign of the mobile app information architecture. Three swipeable screens replace all previous navigation patterns. Every feature is condensed into states within these 3 screens. Settings accessed by tapping the IQ points balance in the persistent header. Design principle: simple shell, jam-packed with features, deeply addictive.
INFORMATION ARCHITECTURE
⚡ SportsIQ
🔥 7-streak

FEATURE CONDENSATION MAP — EVERY OLD SCREEN → NEW HOME
🎯 PICK SCREEN
• Home Live/Idle → Pick Idle + Waiting states
• Pick Card → Pick Active state
• Pick Wait → Pick Waiting state
• Daily Challenge → Pick Daily Challenge state
• Game Day → Pick Game Day state
• Pre-game Brief → Pick Idle intel panel
• Trivia → Daily Challenge state
• Crew Picks → Pick Waiting state
• Multiplier Selector → Pick Active state
• Archetype Edge → Pick Active state
🏆 RESULT SCREEN
• Pick Result → Result Win/Loss states
• Share Card → Result Win state
• Near-miss psychology → Result Loss state
• Prop Coach → Result Loss state (Pro upsell)
• Live game tracker → Result Pending state
👥 LEADERBOARD SCREEN
• Friends Feed → Leaderboard Friends tab
• Global Leaderboard → Leaderboard Global tab
• Bar Crew Mgmt → Leaderboard Venue tab
• Head-to-Head → Leaderboard Challenge tab
• Challenges → Leaderboard Challenge tab
• Referral → Settings Friends tab
• My Stats → Settings Stats tab
• Sports DNA → Settings Profile tab
• SportsIQ Pro → Settings Pro tab
• Notifications → Settings Profile → Notifs
• Onboarding → Pick Idle first-run state
Play Screen — State Logic (confirmed Mar 23)
App opens
→
🧠 Trivia (default)
New game every 30s. Always running.
IF live game detected
→
🏈 Picks mode (replaces trivia)
Trivia hidden until game ends
Game ends
→
🧠 Trivia returns automatically
State A — Trivia (default)
9:41●●●
SportsIQ
⚡ 2,450 IQ
Sports Trivia
22
New game starting in 22 seconds
JM
SK
+9
12 players waiting
10 questions · Sports, Pop Culture, Music
Ready — starts in 22s
No live game. Trivia auto-cycles every 30s. User always has something to do.
State B — Picks (live game replaces trivia)
9:41●●●
SportsIQ
⚡ 2,450 IQ
🏈 Live Game — Trivia paused
ChiefsvsRaiders
Will the Chiefs win tonight's game?
YES — Chiefs win
NO — Raiders win
Signal:
60% YES

Lock In My Pick →
Live game detected. Trivia hidden. Picks take full screen. Trivia returns when game ends.
Bar Experience v1 — Full Evening Flow · Pick · Result · Leaderboard · 2026-03-22
Every screen and state mapped to the bar journey — from walking in to last call. Shows which states activate when, what bar-specific UI layers appear, and how the phone connects to the bar TV display. The bar is SportsIQ's killer context: high emotion, social pressure, real stakes, instant gratification.
BAR NIGHT TIMELINE — 6 MOMENTS, 3 SCREENS
📍
Arrive
Check in
PICK · Idle
🎯
Pick opens
Make your pick
PICK · Active
🔒
Game starts
Watch + track
RESULT · Live
⚔️
Bar battle
Challenge crew
LB · Venue
🏆
Result hits
Win/lose
RESULT · Win
🍺
End of night
Final board
LB · Venue
📺
Bar TV (SportsIQ Barscreen) mirrors the Venue leaderboard throughout the night. Player names appear on the big screen when they make picks and win. The bar TV and phone are in sync — the crowd sees what you're doing. This is the hook that makes strangers engage each other.
BAR LAYER — PERSISTENT ACROSS ALL 3 SCREENS
Once checked in to a venue, every screen gets a bar context strip in the header. Always visible, always live.
⚡ SportsIQ
🔥 7
2,840 IQ ▸
📍 At a sports bar?
Check In
BEFORE CHECK-IN
Home mode · Check-in CTA visible
→
⚡ SportsIQ
🔥 7
2,840 IQ ▸
🍺 Sideline Bar & Grill
23 playing You: #5 📺
Bar Battle active · Top scorer wins a round 🍺 · 2 picks remaining
AFTER CHECK-IN — BAR MODE
Venue strip · Rank · Bar Battle status · TV sync 📺
WHAT BAR MODE ADDS TO EVERY SCREEN
●
Venue strip — always visible below header. Shows bar name, player count, your rank, and 📺 TV sync indicator.
●
Bar Battle status — runs all night. Top scorer at the bar wins a social prize (round of drinks, discount). Creates urgency.
●
📺 TV sync — phone mirrors what's on the bar TV. Your wins and picks appear on the big screen. Public = pressure = engagement.
●
Social pick context — every pick card shows "N others here picked OVER" not just the global crowd %.
MOMENT 1 — ARRIVE AT THE BAR · PICK SCREEN · Idle State
User walks in. App detects nearby venue or they tap check-in. Idle state transforms: upcoming games now show who at the bar is picking them.

Winner announced · Download CTA for non-players
BAR EXPERIENCE DESIGN PRINCIPLES
📱 Phone is the controller
Pick on your phone. Results on your phone. But your name appears on the big screen — creating public stakes that make the game more thrilling and make others want in.
🍺 Bar Battle is the hook
Not just IQ points — a real social prize at the bar. Top scorer wins a round, a discount, recognition. Tangible reward makes every pick feel higher stakes.
📺 TV creates FOMO
Non-players watch the bar TV, see friends winning, see the leaderboard — and want in. The bar TV is SportsIQ's best acquisition tool. Every bar night creates new users.
⚡ Glanceable UI
Bar environment: loud, distracted, beer in one hand. Every screen optimized for 2-second glances. Huge numbers, minimal text, clear color coding (green = winning).
🔄 Loss = immediate next pick
Losses at the bar are the highest-risk churn moment. Redemption pick CTA fires instantly: "Win this → jump from #4 to #2." No wallowing — always a path back.
📅 End of night = next visit hook
Final standings fire a "Rematch?" challenge and a "Defend your title Thursday" reminder. Turns a one-time bar visit into a recurring weekly ritual.
Leaderboard v6 — Social Engine · Friends · Challenges · Referral · 2026-03-22
The Leaderboard is the 3rd swipe screen — the social spine of SportsIQ. This refinement makes the Friends tab a live pick-activity feed with inline challenge buttons, a frictionless friend invite (referral engine), and smart friend suggestions by Sports DNA archetype. The Challenge tab adds H2H duels. Together these create the daily return habit and viral acquisition loop. Every 1 new user who invites 2 friends = 2× growth. This screen is the multiplier.
LEADERBOARD SCREEN — 4 TABS, EACH A DIFFERENT HOOK
Friends tab
Live pick feed from people you know. React, chirp, challenge. See who's hot and who's on a losing streak. Creates daily FOMO — you check the app to see if Rachel blew her pick.
→ Retention driver
Global tab
Competitive context. Where you stand nationally and in your sport. Archetype leaderboards. Hall of fame picks. Creates aspirational ladder to climb.
→ Aspiration driver
Venue tab
Auto-selects when checked in to a bar. Bar Battle standings. Real-time rank among people physically near you. The TV screen mirrors this.
→ Bar engagement
Challenge tab
Active H2H duels. Send challenges, accept or decline. Win a duel = bonus IQ + bragging rights. Creates 1:1 rivalries that keep both users engaged.
→ Session depth
FRIENDS TAB — LIVE PICK ACTIVITY FEED + INVITE ENGINE
The feed that makes people open SportsIQ daily. Live friend activity: who picked what, who won, who's on a streak. React, chirp, challenge inline. Invite CTA when friend list is small — unlocks a real reward (Bar Crew Mode).

DUELS TAB — H2H CHALLENGES · ACTIVE + INCOMING + CREATE
Head-to-head pick challenges. You both pick the same prop — better pick wins bonus IQ. Creates 1:1 rivalries that keep both players returning. "Dave challenged you on the Chiefs pick" = instant re-engagement. Bar mode: challenge the person next to you on the same prop you're both watching.

WHY THE LEADERBOARD SCREEN DRIVES PROFITABILITY
Daily return loop
Friends feed shows live activity. Rachel missed her pick → you feel compelled to check (and chirp). This creates a daily return habit even on non-game days. Target: D30 retention +40% vs. pick-only users.
Referral engine
Bar Crew Mode unlock (requires 3 friends) is the incentive that turns users into recruiters. Personalized link + pre-written message removes friction. Each user that invites 2+ friends = app grows. Target: viral coefficient > 1.0.
Pro conversion
Global tab shows top players are ⚡ Pro with 5× multipliers. The IQ gap is visible. "Top 10 global are all Pro" is the social proof that converts free users. Target: 12% free → Pro conversion from leaderboard screen.

📋 My Picks — Pick History & Stats

Mar 23 2026 · Three views: (1) Active picks currently in play — live score context, friend comparison, time remaining. (2) Pick history feed — resolved picks with win/loss badges, IQ earned/lost, streak markers, and near-miss stat on losses. (3) Stats summary — overall record, accuracy by sport, IQ trend, streak history. This gives Isaac the full data model and UX for the pick history feature, which feeds the Stats panel in the Settings Modal.

🎉 IQ Milestone Celebration — Title Unlock

Mar 23 2026 · The emotional payoff moment when a user crosses a Lifetime IQ threshold (1K → Rookie, 5K → Sharp, 10K → WiseGuy, 25K → Legend, 50K → Elite). Full-screen overlay: title reveal with animation hint, contest tier unlocked, share card, next milestone preview. This is the peak engagement moment — must feel earned. Three milestone states shown: Rookie (1K, day ~7), Sharp (5K, week ~3), WiseGuy (10K, week ~6).

🏆 Contest Entry Flow — From Card to Leaderboard

Mar 23 2026 · Three states: (1) Contest detail bottom sheet — shows prize, eligibility check, live leaderboard preview, urgency timer, and "I'm In" CTA. No IQ is spent — Lifetime IQ minimum is the entry key, picks during the contest window earn contest IQ. (2) Confirmed entry — you're in, urgency to make first pick now to get on the board. (3) Live contest leaderboard — your rank, top players, picks remaining, "closes in X" countdown. Activity gate: Option C (confirmed) — minimum IQ earned from picks in last 30 days (200 IQ/month baseline). Trivia counts. Displayed as "Earned X IQ this month · need 200+" so users understand exactly how to stay eligible.

🌐 Web Referral Flow — Pick Before Download

Mar 23 2026 · Proposed web-first referral approach: the hook (making a pick) happens on mobile web BEFORE the ask (download the app). Tap link → mobile web page (no app needed) → make your pick → pick locked → "See if you won — download the app" → App Store → deep link lands on the game with your pick already registered. This compares against the alternative: app-first flow where you must download before experiencing anything. Three phones show the web-first journey from link-tap to in-app onboarding.

IN-APP AFTER DOWNLOAD
Pick already registered · 500 IQ bonus · MarcD friended · live tracking
WEB-FIRST VS APP-FIRST — WHY WEB-FIRST WINS
✅ Web-first (this design)
• Hook lands at zero friction — pick in 10 seconds, no download needed
• User has skin in the game before ever touching the App Store — they WANT the app to track their pick
• Download CTA is paired with a benefit (track my pick + 500 IQ) not a generic ask
• "MarcD and I are on the same side" is instant social bonding before account creation
• Estimated viral coefficient uplift: 30–50% more installs per referral share
❌ App-first (conventional)
• Ask comes before the experience — "download this" before user has felt anything
• App Store is a choke point — 40–60% of users abandon at the download step
• No skin in the game at download time — curiosity alone must carry the conversion
• Generic onboarding — user forgets MarcD was even involved
• Industry standard: 15–25% referral-to-install rate. Web-first target: 35–45%+

🍺 Bar Night Recap — End of Night Share Card

Mar 23 2026 · Fires at bar closing time (or when user leaves venue). Three states: (1) Bar Champion recap — you won the bar tonight, full celebration, shareable card, rivalry setup for next time. (2) Runner-up recap — close but second place, rivalry hook "next time MarcD, rematch Sunday." (3) Late-joiner recap — only 2 picks, still fun, teaser for next game night. The shareable card drives word-of-mouth and brings friends back to the bar next week. Confirmed in design decisions: "End-of-night recap with shareable card and rivalry hook."

🎯 Pick Detail — Win / Loss / Active States

Mar 23 2026 · Tapping any pick anywhere in the app (history feed, active picks, social feed) opens this full-screen detail view. Three states: (1) Win resolve — celebration overlay, IQ breakdown (base + multiplier), streak badge, share card CTA, "Next pick →" to immediately chain into a new pick. (2) Loss resolve — near-miss stat, "so close" psychology, result facts, "Next pick →" recovery hook. (3) Active/pending — live score progress bar, countdown timer, friend picks on both sides, emoji reaction row (😤🔥😂💀🤝), "Win this → +50 IQ" urgency. This is the emotional core of every pick cycle.

👤 Friend Profile — H2H Rivalry & Social Layer

Mar 23 2026 · Tapping any player name in the app opens this profile view. The H2H record (your wins vs. theirs, head-to-head on the same picks) is the primary switching-cost feature — it accumulates over time and is painful to abandon if you switch apps. Three states: (1) Close friend — full H2H record, recent pick history side-by-side, shared crew membership, emoji reaction history, chirp/challenge CTA. (2) New rival discovered via leaderboard — their stats, mutual friends, "Add Friend" CTA. (3) Your own profile as others see it (same layout, no challenge button).

🧠 Trivia Hub — Broadened Categories

Mar 23 2026 · The full trivia section. Mark confirmed: trivia categories broadened to include non-sports (Pop Culture, Movies/TV, Music, Current Events) so casual fans can compete equally. Daily Challenge = always mixed categories (Mark confirmed "Mixed"). Category trivia lets you play your strongest subject. All trivia feeds the same IQ economy and contest eligibility. Two phones: (1) Daily Challenge day with unplayed state — categories shown as a taste, urgency to play. (2) Category select — choose your round type. Both show the same IQ rewards as picks.

⚡ Pro Paywall v2 — All Confirmed Details

Mar 23 2026 · Updated paywall with all confirmed pricing: $9.99/mo / $79.99/yr ($6.67/mo). Annual plan shown FIRST to anchor the monthly price (confirmed by Mark). 7-day free trial, no charge until day 7. Three states: (1) 5× multiplier gate — modal fires when user taps 5×, shows concrete IQ delta before/after. (2) Sharp Money blur gate — blurred signal + "Sharp signal detected" teaser fires the sheet. (3) Full paywall sheet — all value props, annual/monthly toggle, trial CTA. Design rule: never cold-push Pro upgrade — always fires at a natural moment of desire.

📍 Bar Check-In Flow — From Discovery to Venue Mode

Mar 23 2026 · P5.2 — Three states: (1) Home screen with "Check in to a bar" prompt — appears when GPS detects user near a SportsIQ venue, or via manual tap. (2) Check-in modal — GPS auto-detected venue shown first, or QR scan option. One-tap confirmation. (3) Checked-in state — green venue strip persists across all screens. Bar Battle leaderboard auto-selects in Leaders tab. Venue-level data this generates is the B2B moat: bars see their own engagement dashboard, SportsIQ owns the physical-world social graph. Each checked-in pick = data point tied to specific venue, time, game, crowd. Irreplaceable once 6 months of history accumulates.

🧠 Daily Challenge Card on Home Screen — P5.4

Mar 23 2026 · P5.4 — Isaac spec: compact card sits directly below featured pick, above active picks tray. Tapping opens existing Trivia screen. No new backend needed. Three states: (1) Default — card ready to play, streak shown, category preview, IQ on offer. (2) Streak-at-risk — appears after 9pm if user hasn't played, red urgency pulse, midnight countdown. (3) Post-play — card collapses to a "done" state showing today's score and rank, doesn't vanish so user sees the win. Streak is the addiction engine: 22 days in, missing feels like real loss. The card is permanent daily surface area — every day it either gets played (engagement) or ignored (churn signal).

⚙️ Settings Modal — P5.3

Mar 23 2026 · P5.3 — Isaac spec: tapping the IQ chip in the home screen header slides up a full Settings modal. No new tables needed. Four sections: (1) Stats — pick record, current streak, accuracy %, global rank. (2) Sports preferences — which leagues feed your home screen. (3) Notification toggles — three packs (Game Day, Social, Habit) matching the approved Notifications v4 screen. (4) Account — logout, delete account. Two phones: main settings view (sections collapsed) and sports preferences section expanded. The IQ chip trigger makes settings feel earned/personal rather than buried in a hamburger menu — you tap your score to manage your game.

🚀 Refer-to-Extend Pro — Convert Churn Moments into Growth

Mar 23 2026 · Zero engineering cost to add, zero ad spend to run. When a Pro trial has 3 days or fewer remaining, the "Pro Status" card in Settings transforms from "Manage" into "Refer 1 friend → get 7 more days free." The trial user is at peak motivation (about to lose Pro) — they will share. The friend gets a great first impression. The referrer's trial extends. Every churn moment becomes an acquisition event. Three phones: (1) Settings Pro card at T-3 days with "Extend Free" CTA. (2) Share sheet — pre-filled message anchored to what the user stands to lose ("I'm about to lose 5× multiplier — you should try it before I do"). (3) Extension confirmed — 7 days added, friend showed up. Moat: this mechanic compounds — if 30% of expiring trial users share and 25% of those install, the paywall's K-factor is ~0.075 per trial user, which at scale means hundreds of free installs per month with zero incremental cost.

🗂️ Nav Rename — P5.5 Isaac Spec

Mar 23 2026 · P5.5 — 30-min Isaac task. Rename bottom navigation tabs. Icon changes included. "Prediction" → "Pick" (bolt-outline icon) because the word "prediction" feels formal and hedged; "Pick" is what sports fans actually say. "Leaders" → "Social" (people-outline icon) because the tab contains friends feed, crew activity, and leaderboards — "Social" is the right container word. Trivia and Profile stay unchanged. Before/after shown side-by-side. Tab order stays the same: Home · Pick · [new label] · Social · Profile. This is the last remaining Phase 5 design spec. With P5.1–P5.6 all designed, Isaac has everything he needs to complete Phase 5.

Bottom Nav — Before → After
React Native Expo · Ionicons library · changes in app/(tabs)/ layout
TAB POSITION BEFORE AFTER ICON CHANGE WHY
Tab 1
🏠 Home
🏠 Home (no change)
home-outline → home-outline Stays as-is
Tab 2
🔮 Prediction
⚡ Pick
flash-outline → flash-outline
(or bolt-outline)		 "Prediction" feels formal; sports fans say "Pick"
Tab 3
🧠 Trivia
🧠 Trivia (no change)
brain-outline → brain-outline Stays for now, folds into Pick in Phase 6
Tab 4
🏆 Leaders
👥 Social
trophy-outline → people-outline Tab contains Friends feed, Crew, Leaderboard — "Social" is the right container
Tab 5
👤 Profile
👤 Profile (no change)
person-outline → person-outline Stays as-is
Phase 5 — Design Complete ✓
All P5.1–P5.6 mockups built · Isaac has everything needed to ship Phase 5
✓
P5.1 — Friends + Venue leaderboard tabs → see leaderboard-4tabs screen
✓
P5.2 — Bar Check-In Flow → see bar-checkin-flow screen (4 phones incl. auto-checkout)
✓
P5.3 — Settings Modal → see settings-modal screen (2 phones: main + sports prefs)
✓
P5.4 — Daily Challenge Card on Home → see daily-challenge-home-card screen (3 states)
✓
P5.5 — Nav Rename → THIS screen · 2 changes only (Prediction→Pick, Leaders→Social)
✓
P5.6 — Pro Tier Paywall → see pro-paywall-v2 screen · $9.99/mo or $79.99/yr · 7-day trial
Phase 6 — After Phase 5 Ships
• Swipe navigation (3-screen gesture model)
• Pick resolution poller (auto-resolve when games end, award IQ)
• Push notifications (pick window, result ready, streak at risk)
• Archetype engine (Sports DNA, personalized edge banners)
• Social graph (friend invites, referral system)
• H2H Duels

📊 Bar Owner Dashboard — Venue Analytics & Management

Mar 23 2026 · Web/tablet dashboard that bar owners log into to track engagement, manage their QR code, and see revenue impact. The key missing piece for the distributor pitch — answers "what do I actually get?" This proprietary venue-level data accumulates over time and becomes a switching cost: bar owners won't leave because their historical data is here. Shows tonight's live stats, 7-day player trends, top players at their bar tonight, QR code management, and a revenue impact card comparing to bars without SportsIQ.

Design Notes — Bar Owner Dashboard
• This is the missing piece in the distributor pitch — shows bar owners what they get, not just what players see
• Moat: proprietary venue-level engagement data accumulates over time → switching cost (bar won't leave, their history is here)
• Revenue impact stats (dwell time, repeat visits) are the key conversion metric for bar owner buy-in
• Network rank creates competition between venues → distributors can use this to motivate their bar accounts
• TV Display Controls let bar staff manage the screen without needing technical support
• "Push to Players" + "Bar Tournaments" are Phase 6 features but showing them here seeds demand with bar owners

🏆 Weekly IQ League — Tournament System

Mar 23 2026 · Seasonal competition layer on top of the daily game. Players are placed into tiers (Bronze → Silver → Gold → Diamond) and compete in weekly brackets. Top performers promote up; bottom performers drop down. Resets every Sunday night. Creates a powerful weekly return loop — players have to play to hold their tier. Generates proprietary skill-ranking data no competitor can replicate without the same player history. 4 screens: (1) League Home — your tier + bracket standings, (2) Weekly Bracket — your matchup this week, (3) Tier Promotion — celebration screen when you move up, (4) Season End — recap + prize reveal.

How It Works
• Play any game to earn IQ this week
• Top 4 in your group → promoted Sunday
• Bottom 4 in your group → drop a tier
• Resets every Sunday at midnight

Profile
Moat Analysis — Weekly IQ League
Addiction Loop
Players in the promotion zone can't stop playing — every game could push them into Diamond. Players in relegation zone panic-play to claw back. Both states drive daily return.
Proprietary Data Moat
8+ weeks of skill-ranking data per player. Tier history, accuracy trends, matchup records — this is a permanent competitive advantage that grows every week.
Switching Cost
A Diamond-tier player who built their rank over 3 months won't abandon it to start at Bronze on a competitor app. Tier status becomes identity.
Network Effect
Groups of 20 players compete together. Players recruit friends to "play in my league." Each recruit improves their own relative position — viral growth built into the mechanic.

🎁 IQ Rewards Hub — Prize Store & Redemption

Mar 23 2026 · Closes the loop on "what do IQ points actually win me?" — the first question every new player asks. Two prize tiers: (1) Bar Prizes — sponsored by the venue the player is checked into tonight (free appetizer, drink, merch). Bar owners set the prize and IQ threshold; SportsIQ handles fulfillment notifications. Creates a direct ROI loop for bars. (2) National Prizes — SportsIQ-sponsored gift cards, merch, Pro subscriptions. 3 screens: Prize Store home, Redemption confirmation flow, and Prize history.

IQ Rewards Flywheel — Why This Builds the Moat
🔄
3-Way Flywheel
Players earn IQ → redeem at bars → bars see ROI → bars sponsor bigger prizes → players compete harder → more IQ earned. Each rotation strengthens all three sides.
🏪
Bar Revenue Loop
Bars sponsor an appetizer worth $8. The player stays 40 extra minutes and orders 2 more drinks worth $24. Net positive for the bar every time. Measurable ROI = bars compete to offer better prizes.
🎟
Diamond Prize Aspiration
NFL tickets are only accessible to Diamond-tier players. This makes the IQ League tier system emotionally loaded — your tier now determines what prizes you can even see. Tier = status + access.
For Isaac — Mobile Pick Card + Bar Screens · Mar 25 2026
Mobile: PickCard.tsx as currently implemented on mark/claude-ui — Yes/No choice, 1×/2×/5× multiplier, IQ stakes preview, Lock In. Bar: all 6 states Isaac's BarScreen.tsx needs to render — idle, trivia-q, trivia-rev, predictor-q, predictor-w, predictor-r.

DESIGN LOGIC
Hero zone (top ~75%)
Short-lived moment props. Rotates every 10 min. High urgency — big countdown, dramatic YES/NO cards. Patrons vote on their phones.
Game Stakes strip (bottom)
2–3 game-length props locked in at kickoff. Always visible, updating in real time. Gives every patron a stake in the full game even after moment props expire.
Why both?
Moment props drive dopamine spikes every 10 min. Game stakes create long-arc tension that keeps eyes on the screen all game long.

BAR JOIN FLOW — QR Scan → First Pick · 5 states · Apr 3 2026
Apr 3 2026 · New user scans QR at bar. These are mobile web (PWA) screens, not the native app. Goal: name on the TV leaderboard within 90 seconds of scanning. Specialist insight (Lumen): 60%+ of scans lost to friction — make it one tap. Builder: Isaac needs all 5 states incl. rejoin before he can build auth-free patron entry.

SCAN TO DOWNLOAD

lumen-iq.com/download
WHY THIS SCREEN IS THE MOAT ACTIVATION
State 1-2: Reveal moment
Patron is emotionally primed — they just competed and want to know how they did. This is the highest-attention moment of the night. The loading animation builds anticipation.
State 3: City rank hook
Moving from tonight's bar rank to the city seasonal leaderboard creates a larger identity. "I'm #47 in Houston" is more meaningful than "I was #3 at Bosscat." This is the loyalty trigger.
State 4: Download = moat
Once they download, their rank follows them to every SportsIQ venue. Cross-venue identity data starts accumulating. This is when patron-level data becomes proprietary and compounding.
Bar · Owner Onboarding ✦ — 5-Step Venue Signup
Apr 3 2026 · Isaac dependency unlock. The venue signup flow — how a bar owner signs up, pairs their TV screen, sets up their first game, and goes live. 5 states on web/tablet: (1) Venue details, (2) TV screen pairing, (3) First game config, (4) Plan selection / billing, (5) Go live confirmation. This is the entry point for every new venue — without it Isaac can't wire the venue provisioning flow. Each step is 60 seconds or less. The pairing code (State 2) is the critical moment — bar owner types a 4-digit code shown on their TV to link their account to the screen. State 5 generates the bar's QR code, ready to share or print.
1

2

3

ISAAC BUILD NOTES — VENUE ONBOARDING STATE MACHINE
Step 1 — Venue
POST /venues · fields: name, city, contact_email, phone, sports[ ]. Creates venue row + owner user record. Auth: email magic link or Google OAuth.
Step 2 — TV Pair
TV displays code via GET /screen/pair-code. Bar owner types code → POST /venues/{id}/screens with {code}. Polling: screen pings every 3s. On match: screen gets venue_id + JWT. State: paired ✓
Step 3 — First Game
GET /games/tonight for venue's sports. Owner selects game → POST /venue-games. Sets default config (leaderboard_visible, auto_advance, late_arrivals). Stored on venue_game row.
Step 4 — Billing
Stripe Checkout integration. Plan selection → POST /subscriptions with plan_id. 30-day trial: no card required. trial_ends_at stored on venue. Webhook: subscription.updated → update venue.plan.
Step 5 — Go Live
Generate venue QR: lumen-iq.com/join/{venue_slug}. QR downloadable as PNG. Screen status: GET /screens/{id}/status → live/offline. Redirect to /dashboard/{venue_id} on "Go to Dashboard →"
Bar · Venue Dashboard ✦ — Owner Analytics & Game Control
Apr 3 2026 · B2B retention tool and distributor pitch centerpiece. Web/tablet dashboard that bar owners log into daily. Shows: tonight's live stats, 7-day player trends, top players at the bar, QR code management, and revenue impact vs. bars without SportsIQ. Three views: (1) Tonight Live — real-time player count, active picks, leaderboard snapshot. (2) Trends — 7-day chart, peak nights, player growth, game-by-game engagement. (3) Revenue Impact — comp card vs. baseline, avg tab increase, dwell time lift. The proprietary venue-level data that accumulates here is the switching cost moat: bar owners won't leave because their historical engagement data lives in SportsIQ and nowhere else.

WHY THIS DASHBOARD IS THE B2B MOAT
Switching cost
Every week of data that accumulates here is data a bar owner would lose if they switched. After 6 months, no rational owner leaves.
Demo closer
Graham shows a distributor this dashboard during a sales call. "+$14 avg tab, +47 min dwell time" converts skeptics into buyers in 60 seconds.
Proprietary data
Venue-level engagement data tied to specific games, times, crowds — no competitor can replicate this without running in every bar for years.
Upsell surface
The "Revenue Impact" card justifies the monthly fee every time they open it. $199/mo looks cheap against "+$658 estimated revenue tonight."
